name: Hawaii Loa Ridge Trail
tagline: A steep Ko'olau ridge climb through native forest that delivers summit-caliber views in under 3 miles.
parking: ⚠️ ACCESS RESTRICTED: You must show a valid Hawaii state ID or military ID at the subdivision gate. Only ~10 cars allowed at a time — arrive early or you'll be turned away. Free parking inside once admitted.
hours: Open sunrise to sunset daily; subdivision gate hours may vary
bestTime: Early morning for clear summit views and cooler temps on the exposed upper ridge
talkStory: This ridge holds one of the last intact stands of native lowland forest on Oahu — UH botanists have documented rare lobelias and tree ferns here found almost nowhere else on the island. The trail passes through habitat actively managed to control invasive strawberry guava. You must pass through the gated subdivision where the guard will ask your destination. The upper ridge narrows to a knife-edge with sheer drops into Waimanalo.
